claimText: India is the top recipient country for remittances globally in 2024.
evidenceExcerpt: India is the top recipient country for remittances globally in 2024, ahead of Mexico.
examUse: Statement-based question on India's external sector performance.
distractorHooks: China is the top recipient of remittances globally
Mexico surpassed India in remittance receipts

claimText: The United States is the top source country for remittances to India, and Maharashtra is the top recipient state for the year 2023-24.
evidenceExcerpt: The top source country for remittances to India in 2023-24 was the US (27.7%), and the top recipient state was Maharashtra (20.5%).
examUse: Correct-pair or statement-based question on remittance demographics.
distractorHooks: UAE is the top source country for remittances to India
Kerala is the top recipient state for remittances in India

claimText: Remittances to India surpass gross inward Foreign Direct Investment (FDI), providing a stable source of financing.
evidenceExcerpt: Remittances provide a stable source of financing for India, surpassing gross inward foreign direct investment (FDI).
examUse: Comparison statement in balance of payments and external financing questions.
distractorHooks: Gross inward FDI surpasses remittances to India
Remittances account for less than 5% of gross current account inflows

claimText: The Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) allows resident individuals, including minors, to remit up to USD 250,000 per financial year.
evidenceExcerpt: The Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) allows resident individuals, including minors, to freely remit up to USD 250,000 per financial year.
examUse: Statement-based question on LRS limits and individual eligibility.
distractorHooks: Minors are not eligible for the Liberalised Remittance Scheme
The LRS limit is USD 250,000 per calendar year
The LRS limit is USD 500,000 per financial year

claimText: The Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS) is strictly not available to corporates, partnership firms, Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs), or Trusts.
evidenceExcerpt: The LRS is strictly not available to corporates, partnership firms, Hindu Undivided Families (HUFs), or Trusts.
examUse: Statement-based question identifying eligible and ineligible entities under LRS.
distractorHooks: HUFs and Trusts are eligible to remit money under LRS
Corporates can use LRS up to a specified limit
